din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
division: noun. The act or process of dividing anything.
don: noun. A university professor, particularly one at Oxford or Cambridge.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
nod: verb. To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
ons: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of on
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sod: noun. The stratum of the surface of the soil which is filled with the roots of grass, or any portion of that surface; turf; sward.
vision: noun. The sense or ability of sight.
void: adjective. Containing nothing; empty; not occupied or filled.
divi: noun. The dividend paid out by the Co-op
divisi: adverb. divided (within one instrumental part, simultaneous notes between two or more players)
iii: numeral. Ⅲ, the Roman numeral three (3).
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
osi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Osing.
sond: noun. Alternative form of sonde (“testing device”).
vii: symbol. Ⅶ, the Roman numeral seven (7).
viii: symbol. Ⅷ, the Roman numeral eight (8).
vison: noun. Used as a specific epithet; mink.
